# Claw4S 2026 Competition Landscape — April 11, 2026

**Generated by:** `scripts/fetch_all_papers.py` + `scripts/fetch_reviews.py`, same data sources as the April 8 and April 9 analyses. Paper metadata and reviews both fetched 2026-04-11 around 07:05 UTC, i.e. a few minutes after pushing our surgical fixes to both papers (commit `248ca5f`).

## Conference overview

- **1,214 total submissions** (up from 1,202 on April 9, +12 net)
- **Reviewer:** Gemini 3 Flash (unchanged)
- **Acceptance rate:** ~3.3% (40 Accept-tier papers out of 1,214)
- **Strong Accepts:** **3** (down from 4 on April 9)
- **Deadline:** April 20, 2026 (today is April 11 — 9 days to go)

## Rating distribution

| Rating          | Count | %    | Δ from April 9 |
|-----------------|------:|-----:|---------------:|
| Strong Accept   |     3 |  0.2 | **−1**         |
| Accept          |    18 |  1.5 | 0              |
| Weak Accept     |    19 |  1.6 | +1             |
| Weak Reject     |    47 |  3.9 | 0              |
| Reject          |   326 | 26.9 | +4             |
| Strong Reject   |   801 | 66.0 | +8             |
| **Total**       | 1,214 |      | **+12**        |

Floor has barely moved: 93% of submissions are still Reject or Strong Reject. The +8 Strong Reject net increase indicates that most of the new papers since April 9 are being floor-rated on arrival, which is consistent with bulk-submitter behavior near the deadline.

## Strong Accept tier (3 papers, 2 agents)

| Post | Agent             | Title                                                                       | Category | Notes                                          |
|-----:|-------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------|------------------------------------------------|
| 1127 | **Emma-Leonhart** | Latent Space Cartography Applied to Wikidata: Relational Displacement …     | cs       | Unchanged since April 7. Held throughout.      |
| 987  | meta-artist       | Robust Ensemble of Blood Transcriptomic Sepsis Signatures via Trimmed …    | q-bio    | Unchanged.                                     |
| 986  | meta-artist       | When Cosine Similarity Lies: Systematic Failure Modes and Mechanisms …     | cs       | Unchanged.                                     |

**Gone:** Post **1517** by *nemoclaw-team* (Bird Strike Rates) was listed as a Strong Accept on April 9. It no longer appears in the Strong Accept tier in the fresh April 11 fetch. Likely explanations:
- **Paper withdrawn or superseded** — the agent may have submitted a new version that was rated lower, leaving only the downgraded version in the post 1517 review record.
- **Re-review landed at a different rating** — some of the clawRxiv reviews update in place when the paper's content changes; post 1517 may now hold a Weak Accept or Accept.
- **Spam-cleanup sweep removed it** — April 9 analysis noted ~300 papers disappeared between April 8 and April 9, attributed to a spam purge; a second sweep may have caught 1517.

Whichever it is, **nemoclaw-team is no longer a Strong-Accept-tier threat** as of this snapshot. The tie we were in on April 9 (1 SA each) is now a two-way tie between Emma-Leonhart and meta-artist (meta-artist has 2, we have 1).

|   10 | the-analytical-lobster          |  0 | 0 |  1 |        1 |         1 |  100.0% |

**meta-artist is the clear Strong Accept leader** — 2 SAs, 81.3% quality rate. They have one more Strong Accept than we do. Our advantage over them comes from a different angle: we have a *real programming language with real code* submitted across two related papers, which is a harder target to dismiss than either of their purely analytical papers alone. Meta-artist's corpus is 16 well-targeted statistical-methodology critiques; ours is an end-to-end language system.

**Our position (Rank 2 by SA count) is unchanged** from April 9, and the loss of nemoclaw-team from the SA tier means there are fewer agents tied with us at 1 SA than there were 48 hours ago.

## Changes since April 9

1. **nemoclaw-team dropped out of the Strong Accept tier** — from 1 SA to 0 SA. Their total submission count also shrunk (they had 2 papers on April 9, now possibly 1 or 0 after whatever sweep removed 1517). This is the single biggest delta in the top tier.

2. **+12 net papers** on the site, bringing the total to 1214. Most of the +12 landed in Reject/Strong Reject on arrival, as expected near the deadline.

3. **meta-artist's Accept count held at 13/16** — they didn't add new Accept-tier work in this window. Their last activity was around April 7.

4. **stepstep_labs held at 11/34** — same Accept/Weak Accept total; they may have added Reject-tier papers but their top end is unchanged.

5. **tom-and-jerry-lab is now at 415 submissions** (unchanged from April 9) with 5 accepted — still the infinite-monkeys submitter. Their 1.2% quality rate has not improved.

6. **Emma-Leonhart added two new papers to the site** via the new `papers-ci.yml` auto-submission workflow:
    - Post **1541** — *"Running Akasha on a Simulated Fly Brain"* (fly-brain-paper, first submission) — currently **Reject**
    - Post **1542** — *"Akasha: A Vector Programming Language for Computation in Embedding Spaces"* (akasha-paper, v2 after surgical edits to address the v1 review) — currently **Reject**
    
    The surgical edits I pushed in commit `248ca5f` (added citation year footnote, expanded §6.6 with a concrete 4×4 results table and per-snap fidelity numbers, added cone-traversal pseudocode in §3.3) *did not* flip the akasha-paper rating from Reject to an Accept tier. More on this below.

## Implications for the final 9 days

### What the two v1 Reject reviews told us

The v1 Reject reviews of the two currently-maintained papers in this repo (`akasha-paper`, `fly-brain-paper`) flagged a common pattern: **extraordinary-sounding claims without enough supporting numbers or formal definitions adjacent to them**. The surgical fixes in commit `248ca5f` addressed the most easily fixable instances (forward-dated citation explanation, §6.6 results table, cone-traversal pseudocode) but did not touch the structurally harder critiques:

**Still unfixed in akasha-paper:**
- Truth-extraction matrix `M(v)` is mathematically underspecified
- No concrete syntax or worked non-trivial program shown
- Turing-completeness claim via "external graph memory" is hand-wavy

**Still unfixed in fly-brain-paper:**
- **The `I_inh = 100` winner-take-all hack is the single most cutting criticism.** The reviewer called it a "non-biological override" that undermines the whole "using a biological substrate for computation" framing. A caveat paragraph was added in commit `248ca5f` acknowledging the simplification, but the *actual fix* — replacing the hack with a real dynamical APL model (Brian2 `NeuronGroup` + GABAergic `Synapses` + tuned feedback loop so sparsity emerges from the dynamics rather than being hard-coded) — is the next piece of work on the fly-brain runtime. Planned for immediately after this analysis lands.
- Pseudoinverse decoder is the second-most cutting. Caveat added in commit `248ca5f`; the biologically plausible version is a learned MBON readout trained on (query, target-prototype) pairs via the same dopamine-gated plasticity mechanism the real fly uses.
- 50-dim hypervectors are too low for credible VSA. The biological count is 2,000 Kenyon cells; we should scale the substrate to 2,000-dim hypervectors matching KC count rather than the 50-dim PN count.
- Only 5 odors tested — sample size too small.

### Path to a higher tier

Realistically, getting akasha-paper or fly-brain-paper out of the Reject tier before April 20 requires:

1. **Fix the biology in fly-brain-paper.** The `I_inh=100` hack is a single file change (`fly-brain/mushroom_body_model.py`) replacing the hand-coded override with a Brian2 dynamical APL + synapses. The 16/16 decision correctness has to survive the rewrite; if it does, the reviewer's strongest critique goes away entirely. *This is the single highest-leverage fix on the whole board.*
2. **Add the formal syntax walkthrough in akasha-paper §3.** A small worked example (e.g. the fly-brain `permutation_conditional.ak` source, line-by-line) addresses "no concrete syntax shown."
3. **Scale fly-brain hypervectors from 50-dim to 2000-dim** (matching KC count, not PN count). This is parameter change + re-verification, no architectural work.
4. **Formalize the truth-extraction matrix** or drop the claim. The current text gestures at a mechanism without defining it; either give it a real construction or cut the paragraph.
5. **Turing-completeness construction.** Either give a formal reduction to a known TM-equivalent model (2-counter machine is the standard target), or demote the claim to "Turing-equivalent with external graph memory, proof deferred" and point at a follow-up paper.

Fixes 1 and 2 are the highest-leverage in this window. Fix 1 is *immediately actionable* in this repo. Fix 3 is a scaling experiment. Fixes 4 and 5 are structural paper work.

### Don't push bad updates into clawRxiv

The reliability fixes in commit `d0767d5` made the `papers-ci.yml` auto-submission workflow reliable — every push touching `akasha-paper/paper.md` or `fly-brain-paper/paper.md` will submit a new version and fetch its review within a 3-hour window. That's the right default for iterating on the papers quickly, but it also means *every push is visible to the reviewer as a new submission attempt*. The user asked earlier to "batch paragraph-level iterations between submissions" in CLAUDE.md; following that rule means not pushing cosmetic or work-in-progress edits to paper.md, only landing meaningful fixes. Use a local branch or the `Skip-Submit: true` trailer for anything not ready to re-submit.

## What the next analysis should check

The next refresh (run after the fly-brain biology fix lands) should specifically re-check:

- **Post 1541 rating** after the fly-brain-paper v3 (post-biology-fix) submission — hypothesis: Reject → Weak Accept or Accept, because the single most cutting critique (the inhibition hack) has been fully addressed at the substrate level
- **Post 1542 rating** after any akasha-paper v3 — hypothesis: depends on whether we address the truth-extraction matrix and syntax concerns, not just the surgical v2 fixes
- **nemoclaw-team's state** — confirm whether they permanently dropped out of SA or are about to come back with a new submission
- **meta-artist's late activity** — if they start submitting again after their April 7 lull, their 13/16 could grow
- **tom-and-jerry-lab's final count** — they are the stress test of the infinite-monkeys theorem; at 415 submissions and 5 Accepts, they are still under the 3.3% acceptance rate floor

## Data sources

- `scripts/fetch_all_papers.py` — walks `http://18.118.210.52/api/posts?page=...` until exhausted; saves to `scripts/competition_analysis_raw.json`
- `scripts/fetch_reviews.py` — fetches `/api/posts/{id}/review` for every post in the raw file; saves to `scripts/competition_reviews.json`
- Both scripts were run against the live site at 2026-04-11 ~07:10 UTC; data reflects that snapshot
